figures.caption: Figure 3: Six scenarios defining the collision behavior measure mc. Each causes the ego car (dark gray) to crash within two timesteps. Columns correspond to the action taken: LEFT, RIGHT, or FASTER.
Table 2: Examples of expressing behaviors in different domains as a measure m(π).

Figure 2: Left: Return breakdown of a PPO agent training on HighJax. Training is deliberately slowed down to DKL = 5 × 10−4 per epoch. Right: Value of the collision behavior measure mc across training.

Table 4: Observation matrices for the six scenarios defining the collision behavior measure mc (Eq. 4). Each matrix O ∈ R5×5 contains the ego vehicle (absolute) and four nearest NPCs (relative). Columns: presence, longitudinal position, lateral position, longitudinal velocity, lateral velocity.
Table 1: A cross-taxonomy of XRL methods according to their explanation targets. The purple cells indicate the corresponding categories in three surveys.
